prs.title: feat: [Issue 5] Implement Git repository cloner and file discovery
prs.body:
Resolves #5

### Description
This PR implements the first part of the Repository Ingestion module, specifically focusing on the `RepoCloner` class required for the AST Parsing Engine.

### Changes Made
- **Created Cloner Module:** Added `src/reporag/ingestion/cloner.py` with the `RepoCloner` class.
- **Shallow Cloning:** Implemented `clone_and_discover` which performs a shallow clone (`depth=1`) using `GitPython` to vastly improve clone performance.
- **Resource Management:** Handles temporary directory creation automatically, including robust cleanup on clone failures using `tempfile.TemporaryDirectory()`.
- **File Discovery:** Added a file discovery tree-walker that filters out `.git` metadata and only parses supported language extensions (`.py`, `.js`, `.ts`).
- **Manifest Generation:** Successfully generates a manifest list containing `(file_path, language, size_bytes)` for every supported file as requested.
- **Code Quality:** Formatted strictly with `black` and linted with `ruff`.

prs.title: [Issue 4] Project configuration module: .env loading and settings validation
prs.body:
---

### PR #44 — Issue 4


## Summary

Closes #4

## What changed

- Created `src/reporag/config.py` with a centralized `Settings` class using `pydantic-settings` (`BaseSettings`), organized into logical sections: App, Database, Neo4j, Qdrant, LLM, Auth, and OAuth.
- Marked sensitive fields (`NEO4J_PASSWORD`, `JWT_SECRET`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `QDRANT_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`) as `SecretStr` to prevent accidental logging or serialization of secrets.
- The `settings` singleton is initialized at module level, so any missing required variables immediately raise a `ValidationError` at import time — the app refuses to start rather than failing silently later.
- Updated `.env.example` with fully documented variables and inline comments. Required secrets are commented out by default so that copying the template without editing it correctly triggers validation errors.

## How to test

```bash
# Test 1: Verify validation catches missing required vars
cp .env.example .env
python -c "from src.reporag.config import settings; print(settings.model_dump())"
# Expected: ValidationError for NEO4J_PASSWORD and JWT_SECRET

# Test 2: Verify settings load correctly when all required vars are set
echo "NEO4J_PASSWORD=test123" >> .env
echo "JWT_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)" >> .env
python -c "from src.reporag.config import settings; print(settings.model_dump())"
# Expected: Full settings dict printed successfully

prs.title: [Issue 3] Add database models and session factory
prs.body:
---

### PR #43 — Issue 3

## Summary

Closes #3

## What changed

- Created `src/reporag/db/models.py` with four SQLAlchemy ORM models: `User`, `Repository`, `IngestionJob`, and `QueryLog`, including foreign keys, cascading relationships, and `server_default` timestamps.
- Created `src/reporag/db/session.py` with an async session factory (`get_db`) that dynamically selects between SQLite (default) and PostgreSQL based on the `DATABASE_URL` environment variable. Automatically rewrites `postgresql://` to `postgresql+asyncpg://` for async driver compatibility.
- Initialized Alembic with the async template and configured `alembic/env.py` to import our `Base.metadata` and use `get_database_url()` for dynamic database connection.
- Auto-generated the initial migration (`Initial schema`) that creates all four tables.
- Added `greenlet` to `requirements.txt` as a required dependency for SQLAlchemy's async migrations.

## How to test

```bash
# Run migrations locally (creates reporag.db with SQLite)
alembic upgrade head

# Verify tables were created
sqlite3 reporag.db ".tables"
# Expected output: alembic_version  ingestion_jobs  query_logs  repositories  users

# Verify session factory imports correctly
python -c "from src.reporag.db.session import get_db; print('DB OK')"

prs.title: [Issue 2] Configure and fix pre-commit hooks
prs.body:
---

### PR #42 — Issue 2


## Summary

Closes #2

## What changed

- Configured `.pre-commit-config.yaml` with hooks for: trailing whitespace, end-of-file fixer, YAML validation, large file check, ruff linting, black formatting, ASCII-only guard, and internal data guard.
- Fixed the ASCII-only guard hook entry to use a YAML block scalar (`>`) to prevent YAML parsing errors caused by colons inside inline bash commands.
- Fixed the internal data guard hook with the same YAML block scalar approach.
- Added a dummy unit test (`tests/unit/test_dummy.py`) to prevent `pytest` exit code 5 when no real tests exist yet.

## How to test

```bash
pre-commit install
pre-commit run --all-files
# All hooks should pass

prs.title: [Issue 1] Initialise repo scaffold, CI workflow, Docker setup
prs.body:
---

### PR #41 — Issue 1

## Summary

Closes #1

## What changed

- Created the GitHub Actions CI workflow (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) with jobs for ASCII guard, internal data guard, lint & format (ruff + black), and unit tests.
- Added `docker-compose.yml` with services for the API, Neo4j, Qdrant, and PostgreSQL — all with health checks and persistent volumes.
- Created `Dockerfile` for the FastAPI application using `python:3.11-slim`.
- Added a dummy unit test (`tests/unit/test_dummy.py`) to prevent `pytest` from returning exit code 5 (no tests collected) and failing CI.
- Fixed the `.gitignore` pathspec issue that was causing `git` errors on certain platforms.

## How to test

```bash
# Start Docker infrastructure
docker compose up --build -d
docker compose ps   # all services should be healthy

# Run CI checks locally
ruff check src/ tests/
black --check src/ tests/
pytest tests/unit/ -v

prs.title: [Issue 2] Configure and fix pre-commit hooks
prs.body:
## Summary

Closes #2

## What changed

- Fixed a YAML syntax error in the `internal-data-guard` bash script.
- Replaced the `ascii-only` `grep -P` check with a cross-platform `perl` command to ensure compatibility with macOS.
- Downgraded `black` to `24.4.2` to bypass the memory safety block on Python 3.12.5.

## How to test

```bash
pre-commit install
pre-commit run --all-files

prs.title: add Quantum Portal
prs.body:
##Hacktoberfest 2025 Contribution
Animation Submission: Quantum Portal
Directory: Kavya-Jain-coder-quantum-portal

##Description
This PR adds a spectacular quantum portal animation with multi-dimensional rotating rings, a pulsing energy core, and floating particles. The animation features:

Five concentric rings rotating in different 3D planes
Multi-axis 3D rotations creating interdimensional effect
Pulsing energy core with intense radial glow
Eight floating particles orbiting the portal
Vibrant neon colors (magenta, cyan, yellow, green, orange)
Layered box-shadow effects creating depth and luminosity

##Animation Features
Five rings rotate independently at different speeds (2s-4s cycles)
Each ring has unique 3D orientation (rotateX, rotateY, rotateZ combinations)
Rings alternate rotation direction (clockwise/counter-clockwise)
Central core pulses with scale and opacity animation
Eight particles float in various directions with unique paths
Entire portal container rotates on Y-axis (20s cycle)
Dual box-shadow on each ring (outer + inset glow)
Triple-layered glow on portal core
Particles fade in/out during movement (opacity 0.5-1)
Pure CSS with transform-style: preserve-3d for true 3D depth

##Files Included
index.html - Main HTML structure with portal components
styles.css - All styling, 3D transforms, and animations
meta.json - Metadata configuration

## Hacktoberfest
This PR is submitted as part of Hacktoberfest 2025. Please consider adding the hacktoberfest-accepted label if this contribution meets the project's standards.

##Preview
The animation displays a mesmerizing quantum portal with five colorful glowing rings spinning in multiple dimensions. Each ring rotates on different 3D axes, creating a complex, interdimensional gateway effect. At the center, a brilliant white energy core pulses with intense light. Eight small particles orbit the portal, floating in various directions. The entire structure rotates slowly, revealing the full 3D complexity of the portal. The radial gradient background enhances the cosmic atmosphere.

##Technical Highlights
Multi-plane Rotation: Each ring rotates on unique X/Y/Z axis combinations
Preserve-3D Cascading: Container and rings maintain 3D space
Reverse Animations: Alternate rings use reverse direction
Dual Box-shadow: Outer glow + inset glow for depth
Radial Core Gradient: White → Purple → Blue transparency
Staggered Timing: Rings spin at 2s, 2.5s, 3s, 3.5s, 4s durations
Particle Choreography: Four unique floating patterns
Scale Animation: Core pulses from 1.0 to 1.2
Perspective View: 1000px perspective creates depth
##Ring Configuration
Ring 1 (Outermost - 400px):

Color: Magenta (rgba(255, 0, 255, 0.8))
Rotation: rotateX(75deg) + rotateZ spin
Speed: 4 seconds
Direction: Clockwise
Ring 2 (340px):

Color: Cyan (rgba(0, 255, 255, 0.8))
Rotation: rotateY(75deg) + rotateZ spin
Speed: 3.5 seconds
Direction: Counter-clockwise (reverse)
Ring 3 (280px):

Color: Yellow (rgba(255, 255, 0, 0.8))
Rotation: rotateX(45deg) rotateY(45deg) + rotateZ spin
Speed: 3 seconds
Direction: Clockwise
Ring 4 (220px):

Color: Green (rgba(0, 255, 128, 0.8))
Rotation: rotateX(60deg) rotateY(30deg) + rotateZ spin
Speed: 2.5 seconds
Direction: Counter-clockwise (reverse)
Ring 5 (Innermost - 160px):

Color: Orange (rgba(255, 128, 0, 0.8))
Rotation: rotateX(30deg) rotateY(60deg) + rotateZ spin
Speed: 2 seconds
Direction: Clockwise
##Portal Core
Size: 100px diameter
Colors: White → Purple → Blue (radial gradient)
Glow: Triple-layer (60px, 100px, 140px spread)
Animation: 2s pulse cycle
Scale: 1.0 to 1.2
Opacity: 1.0 to 0.8
Z-index: 10 (front layer)
##Particle System (8 particles)
Float Patterns:

Float1: Translate(30px, -30px) - diagonal up-right
Float2: Translate(-30px, 30px) - diagonal down-left
Float3: Translate(30px, 30px) - diagonal down-right
Float4: Translate(-30px, -30px) - diagonal up-left
Timing:

Durations: 5s to 7s per cycle
Opacity: Fades 1.0 → 0.5 → 1.0
Size: 8px diameter white spheres
Glow: 10px white box-shadow

prs.title: add Morphing Shapes
prs.body:
##Hacktoberfest 2025 Contribution
Animation Submission: Morphing Shapes
Directory: Kavya-Jain-coder-morphing-shapes

##Description
This PR adds a mesmerizing morphing shape animation with 3D rotation and dynamic gradient effects. The animation features:

Fluid shape morphing through 10+ unique organic forms
Multi-axis 3D rotation (X, Y, Z axes simultaneously)
Animated gradient background shifting through 5 colors
Dynamic border-radius creating liquid-like transitions
Layered glow effects with box-shadow
Smooth, continuous transformation over 12-second cycle


##Animation Features

Single shape morphs through 11 distinct forms (0%-100% keyframes)
Complex border-radius values create organic, blob-like shapes
3D rotation on all three axes (rotateX, rotateY, rotateZ)
Five-color gradient animates across the shape surface
Dual animation timing: 12s morph cycle + 8s gradient shift
Glowing effects with multi-layer box-shadow
Inset shadow adds depth and dimension
Pseudo-element ::before creates the visible colored surface
Perspective view enhances 3D rotation effect
Pure CSS with transform-style: preserve-3d


##Files Included

index.html - Main HTML structure
styles.css - All styling, morphing, and 3D animations
meta.json - Metadata configuration

##Hacktoberfest
This PR is submitted as part of Hacktoberfest 2025. Please consider adding the hacktoberfest-accepted label if this contribution meets the project's standards.

##Preview
The animation displays a vibrant, shape-shifting blob that continuously morphs through various organic forms while rotating in 3D space. The shape flows seamlessly from circle to asymmetric blobs, wave-like forms, and back again. A five-color gradient (red, cyan, blue, yellow, purple) animates across its surface, while glowing halos pulse around it. The entire sequence creates a hypnotic, lava-lamp-like effect against a purple gradient background.

##Technical Highlights

Complex Border-radius: Uses 8-value syntax (horizontal / vertical) for each corner
Multi-axis 3D Rotation: Simultaneous rotateX, rotateY, rotateZ transforms
Preserve-3D: Maintains 3D space throughout transformation
Pseudo-element Surface: ::before creates the colored morphing surface
Gradient Animation: 300% background-size with position animation
Triple Box-shadow: Outer glow + far glow + inset highlight
11 Morph Stages: Smooth transitions between unique shapes
Synchronized Timing: 12s morph, 12s 3D rotation, 8s gradient shift


##Morph Sequence (12s cycle)

0% - Circle: Perfect 50% border-radius
10% - Wave: Asymmetric 30/70 split
20% - Organic blob: 58/42/75/25 values
30% - Twisted form: 50/50/33/67 pattern
40% - Complex shape: Mixed asymmetric values
50% - Diamond-ish: 20/80 alternating pattern + 180° rotation
60% - Flowing form: 67/33/47/53 values
70% - Stretched blob: 79/21 extreme values
80% - Balanced form: 48/52/30/70 pattern
90% - Wave variation: 70/30 corners
100% - Back to circle: Returns to perfect circle + 360° rotation


##Color Palette
Gradient Colors:

Red (#ff6b6b)
Cyan (#4ecdc4)
Sky blue (#45b7d1)
Yellow (#f7dc6f)
Purple (#bb8fce)

Background:

Purple gradient (#667eea → #764ba2 → #f093fb)

Glow Effects:

Red glow (rgba(255, 107, 107, 0.6))
Cyan glow (rgba(78, 205, 196, 0.4))
White inset highlight

prs.title: add cyberpunk city
prs.body:
##Hacktoberfest 2025 
##Description
This PR adds an immersive cyberpunk cityscape animation with neon-lit buildings, falling rain, and a flickering neon sign. The animation features:
Six futuristic skyscrapers with varying heights and neon accents
Animated window lights blinking in multiple neon colors
Eight falling rain streaks creating atmospheric rainfall
Flickering neon sign with intense glow effect
Purple gradient sky setting the cyberpunk mood
Cyan lighting effects on building edges and rain

##Animation Features
Six buildings with unique heights (350px-500px) and positions
Each building has cyan-lit top edge with glowing box-shadow
Windows animated with horizontal neon strips (magenta, cyan, yellow)
Window lights blink/pulse with 3s opacity cycle
Eight rain drops fall continuously from top to bottom
Staggered rain timing (0s-0.6s delays) creates natural rainfall
Neon sign "CYBER" flickers realistically with shadow cutouts
Multi-layered text-shadow creates intense neon glow
Rain gradient fades from transparent to cyan
Dark building silhouettes contrast with vibrant neon accents
Pure CSS - no JavaScript or images required

##Files Included

index.html - Main HTML structure with city elements
styles.css - All styling, neon effects, and animations
meta.json - Metadata configuration

##Hacktoberfest
This PR is submitted as part of Hacktoberfest 2025. Please consider adding the hacktoberfest-accepted label if this contribution meets the project's standards.🖼️ Preview
The animation displays a stunning cyberpunk cityscape at night with six dark skyscrapers silhouetted against a purple gradient sky. Each building is topped with a glowing cyan edge and features colorful horizontal window strips in magenta, cyan, and yellow that pulse rhythmically. Cyan rain streaks fall continuously across the scene at varying speeds. Dominating the upper portion, a massive neon "CYBER" sign flickers dramatically with intense magenta glow, creating an authentic retro-futuristic atmosphere reminiscent of Blade Runner.💡 Technical Highlights

Pseudo-element Windows: ::before creates multi-gradient window pattern
Layered Gradients: 8 horizontal gradients simulate building windows
Text-shadow Glow: 7 layers of shadow create intense neon effect
Flicker Animation: Strategic keyframe timing creates realistic power surge
Rain Gradient: Transparent-to-cyan creates motion blur effect
Transform Performance: Uses translateY for smooth rain animation
Staggered Delays: Varied animation-delay creates natural randomness
Box-shadow Ambient: Cyan glow on building tops adds atmosphere


##Scene Components
Buildings (6):

Widths: 90px-140px
Heights: 350px-500px (varying skyline)
Color: Dark navy gradient (#1a1a2e to #16213e)
Top border: 3px cyan (#0ff) with glow
Windows: 8 horizontal neon strips per building
Window Colors:

Magenta (#ff00ff) - 10%, 40%, 70%
Cyan (#00ffff) - 20%, 50%, 80%
Yellow (#ffff00) - 30%, 60%
3s pulse cycle (opacity 1 to 0.5)
Rain Drops (8):

Width: 2px (thin streaks)
Height: 50px
Color: Cyan gradient fade
Speed: 1 second fall duration
Positions: 10%-85% across viewport
Delays: 0s-0.6s for natural effect
Neon Sign:

Text: "CYBER"
Size: 80px bold
Color: Magenta (#ff00ff)
Glow: 7 layers (10px-100px spread)
Flicker: Realistic power surge pattern

Directory: Kavya-Jain-coder-aurora-borealis

##Description
This PR adds a breathtaking Aurora Borealis (Northern Lights) animation with flowing light waves, twinkling stars, and mountain silhouettes. The animation features:

Three layered aurora waves with flowing, organic movement
Twinkling starfield creating a realistic night sky
Mountain silhouettes providing landscape depth
Flowing wave animations with skew and translation effects
Gradient color transitions mimicking real aurora colors
Screen blend mode for authentic light blending

Three independent aurora layers with different colors and timing
Each aurora wave moves with unique skew and translation patterns
Aurora colors include green, blue, cyan, purple, and pink hues
Starfield created with multiple radial gradients for depth
Stars twinkle with opacity animation (3s cycle)
Three mountain silhouettes in foreground for composition
Heavy blur effect (40px) creates soft, ethereal aurora glow
Mix-blend-mode: screen creates realistic light overlay
Smooth, continuous animations (15s, 18s, 20s cycles)
Pure CSS with no JavaScript or images required

styles.css - All styling, animations, and visual effects

##Hacktoberfest
This PR is submitted as part of Hacktoberfest 2025. Please consider adding the hacktoberfest-accepted label if this contribution meets the project's standards.

##Preview

Three layers of aurora waves flow gracefully with undulating, wave-like motions in vibrant shades of green, blue, purple, and pink. Above, countless stars twinkle against the deep blue-black gradient sky. Below, three mountain silhouettes create a dramatic foreground, completing this immersive natural phenomenon visualization.

##Technical Highlights

Multiple Radial Gradients: Creates realistic starfield with varied star sizes
Filter Blur: 40px blur creates soft, diffused aurora effect
Mix-blend-mode: Screen mode allows aurora layers to blend naturally
Skew Transforms: Creates wave-like distortion in aurora movement
Multi-phase Animation: Different keyframe timings create organic flow
Gradient Opacity: Transparent edges fade aurora naturally
CSS Triangles: Border technique creates mountain silhouettes
Layered Composition: Proper z-index stacking for depth


##Animation Layers
Starfield:

8 radial gradient stars per tile
200px × 200px repeating pattern
Varying sizes (1px and 2px)
3s twinkle cycle (opacity 1 to 0.7)

Aurora Layer 1:

Colors: Green (#00ff96) → Cyan → Blue → Purple
Duration: 15 seconds
Movement: -50% to +30% translateX with skew
Top position: 10%

Aurora Layer 2:

Colors: Mint → Purple → Pink
Duration: 18 seconds
Movement: +50% to -30% translateX (opposite direction)
Top position: 15%

Aurora Layer 3:

Colors: Cyan → Purple gradient
Duration: 20 seconds
Movement: 0% to ±40% translateX
Top position: 20%

Mountains (3):

Mountain 1: 600px base, 400px height (left)
Mountain 2: 800px base, 500px height (center)
Mountain 3: 700px base, 450px height (right)

Three clock hands rotate at different speeds, each with its own neon color matching the rings. The rings pulse and scale independently, creating a hypnotic cyberpunk effect. A bright white center dot serves as the clock's pivot point, while a radial gradient glow adds atmospheric depth.

prs.title: add Spinning Loader
prs.body:
##Hacktoberfest 2025 Contribution
Animation Submission: Spinning Loader
Directory: your-github-username-spinning-loader

##Description
This PR adds a sophisticated multi-layered spinning loader animation with three concentric circles. The animation features:

Three nested spinning circles with different sizes and colors
Multi-directional rotation (clockwise and counter-clockwise)
Varying animation speeds for dynamic visual effect
Smooth, continuous motion with no stuttering
Colorful design with complementary color scheme


##Animation Features

Three concentric circles rotating at different speeds
Outer circle (red) rotates at 2 seconds per revolution
Middle circle (cyan) rotates in reverse at 1.5 seconds per revolution
Inner circle (yellow) rotates fastest at 1 second per revolution
Container itself rotates for added complexity
Each circle shows only top border for spinner effect
Pure CSS animations - no JavaScript required
Infinite loop for continuous loading indication


##Files Included

index.html - Main HTML structure with three circle elements
styles.css - All styling and multi-layered animations
meta.json - Metadata configuration



##Hacktoberfest
This PR is submitted as part of Hacktoberfest 2025. Please consider adding the hacktoberfest-accepted label if this contribution meets the project's standards.

##Preview
The animation displays three concentric spinning circles in vibrant colors (red, cyan, and yellow) against a beautiful purple gradient background. Each circle rotates at a different speed, with the middle circle spinning in the opposite direction, creating a mesmerizing, hypnotic effect. The entire loader container also rotates slowly, adding an extra layer of visual complexity.

##Technical Highlights

Concentric Layout: Three perfectly centered circles using absolute positioning
Multi-Speed Animation: Different durations (2s, 1.5s, 1s) create dynamic motion
Reverse Animation: Middle circle uses reverse direction for counter-rotation
Border Technique: Only top border visible creates classic spinner effect
Nested Rotations: Container rotation + individual circle rotations
Color Harmony: Red (#ff6b6b), Cyan (#4ecdc4), Yellow (#ffe66d)


##Animation Layers

Outer Circle (200px) - Red, 2s rotation, clockwise
Middle Circle (140px) - Cyan, 1.5s rotation, counter-clockwise
Inner Circle (80px) - Yellow, 1s rotation, clockwise
Container (200px) - 3s rotation, adds overall spinning effect

prs.title: add rainbow wave
prs.body:
##Hacktoberfest 2025 Contribution
Animation Submission: Rainbow Wave
Directory: Kavya-Jain-coder-rainbow-wave

##Description
This PR adds a vibrant rainbow wave animation with colorful bars that create a mesmerizing wave pattern. The animation features:
Eight rainbow-colored bars (ROYGBIV spectrum + pink)
Sequential wave motion with perfectly timed delays
Smooth height transitions creating a flowing wave effect
Dark background for maximum color contrast
Rounded bar edges for modern, polished look


##Animation Features

Eight vertical bars in rainbow colors (red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, violet, pink)
Each bar grows and shrinks with staggered timing
Creates a continuous, flowing wave pattern
Smooth easing for natural, fluid motion
Perfectly synchronized animation delays (0.15s intervals)
Pure CSS animations - no JavaScript required
Infinite loop for continuous visual effect


##Files Included

styles.css - All styling, colors, and animations
meta.json - Metadata configuration



##Hacktoberfest
This PR is submitted as part of Hacktoberfest 2025. Please consider adding the hacktoberfest-accepted label if this contribution meets the project's standards.

##Preview
The animation displays eight colorful vertical bars arranged in a row, each in a different rainbow color. The bars grow and shrink in sequence, creating a smooth, flowing wave pattern that travels from left to right. The dark background (#1a1a2e) makes the vibrant rainbow colors pop beautifully.

##Technical Highlights

Rainbow Spectrum: Full ROYGBIV color palette plus pink accent
Precise Timing: 0.15s delay intervals create perfect wave motion
Height Animation: Bars grow from 50px to 180px for dramatic effect
Border Radius: 10px rounded corners for modern aesthetic
Flexbox Layout: Proper alignment and spacing between bars
Animation Synchronization: Carefully timed delays for fluid wave

Indigo (#4b0082) - Deep purple

prs.title: add pulsing heart
prs.body:
##Hacktoberfest 2025 Contribution
Animation Submission: Pulsing Heart
Directory: Kavya-Jain-Coder-pulsing-heart

##Description
This PR adds a beautiful pulsing heart animation created entirely with CSS. The animation features:

Pure CSS heart shape using pseudo-elements
Realistic heartbeat rhythm with double-pulse pattern
Smooth scaling animation for lifelike beating effect
Vibrant gradient background (pink to blue)
No images or SVG required - all CSS shapes


##Animation Features

Heart shape constructed using ::before and ::after pseudo-elements
Double-pulse rhythm mimics a real heartbeat
Scales up twice per cycle for authentic beating pattern
Smooth easing creates natural, organic motion
Bright red (#ff4757) heart color for visual impact
Pure CSS - no JavaScript, images, or external assets
Infinite loop for continuous heartbeat effect


##Files Included

index.html - Main HTML structure
styles.css - All styling, shape creation, and animations
meta.json - Metadata configuration



##Hacktoberfest
This PR is submitted as part of Hacktoberfest 2025. Please consider adding the hacktoberfest-accepted label if this contribution meets the project's standards.

##Preview
The animation displays a vibrant red heart that pulses with a realistic double-beat rhythm against a stunning pink-to-blue gradient background. The heart beats continuously, scaling up and down to create a lifelike heartbeat effect that's both visually appealing and emotionally engaging.

##Technical Highlights

Pseudo-element Mastery: Heart shape built with ::before and ::after
Transform Origins: Proper rotation points for perfect heart shape
Double Pulse: 25% and 75% keyframes create realistic heartbeat rhythm
Border Radius: Rounded tops create smooth, organic curves
Scale Transform: Smooth size changes for beating effect

Thank you for maintaining this awesome repository and for considering my contribution! Happy Hacktoberfest! 🎃💖

prs.title: rotating cube
prs.body:
##Hacktoberfest 2025 Contribution
Animation Submission: Rotating Cube
Directory: Kavya-Jain-coder-rotating-cube

##Description
This PR adds a mesmerizing 3D rotating cube animation with vibrant colored faces. The animation features:

Full 3D transformation using CSS 3D transforms
Six uniquely colored faces (red, green, blue, yellow, magenta, cyan)
Smooth continuous rotation on multiple axes
Proper perspective rendering for realistic depth
Semi-transparent faces for enhanced visual effect


##Animation Features

True 3D cube constructed with CSS transforms
Rotates simultaneously on X and Y axes
Each face has a distinct color for easy tracking
Smooth 8-second rotation cycle
Uses transform-style: preserve-3d for proper 3D rendering
Perspective depth creates realistic spatial effect
Pure CSS - no JavaScript or libraries required


##Files Included

index.html - Main HTML structure with cube faces
styles.css - All styling, 3D transforms, and animations
meta.json - Metadata configuration



##Hacktoberfest
This PR is submitted as part of Hacktoberfest 2025. Please consider adding the hacktoberfest-accepted label if this contribution meets the project's standards.

##Preview
The animation displays a 3D cube with six different colored faces rotating continuously in 3D space. The cube rotates on both X and Y axes simultaneously, showcasing all faces as it spins. The semi-transparent faces with white borders create a modern, polished look against the blue gradient background.

##Technical Highlights

3D Perspective: Proper perspective values for realistic depth
Transform-style: Preserves 3D positioning of child elements
Multi-axis Rotation: Simultaneous X and Y rotation for dynamic movement
Color Coding: Each face uniquely colored for visual interest
Smooth Animation: Linear timing function for constant rotation speed



Thank you for maintaining this awesome repository and for considering my contribution! Happy Hacktoberfest! 🎃
